content:
My Book Recommendations Books I have already read Love and Math - Edward Frenkel Only read if you have some university math knowledge! Read if interested in Group Theory! Read if interested in academic antisemitism in the late Soviet Union Books I am going to read Joan Didion - Play It as It Lies
Dr. Kevin Dutton - The Wisdom of Psychopaths Steven Novella - The Skeptics’ Guide to the Universe William Egginton - The Rigor of Angels Jacob L. Wright - Why the Bible Began The Emperor of All Maladies - Siddhartha Mukherjee Flow - Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi The Diary of a CEO - Steven Bartlett Thucydides Trap - Graham Allison Investing Amid Low Expected Returns - Antti Ilmanen Freakonomics - Levitt, Dubner The girl on the train - Paula Hawkins Elliptic Tales - Avner Ash When Genius Failed - Roger Lowenstein
title: Book Recommendations

title: CV

content:
About Hi! My name is Leopold Goedhart and I am currently a B.Sc. student @ the physics department of ETH in Zürich (Switzerland).
I was born and raised in Hamburg (Germany) and visited elementary and high school there. In July 2018 I graduated and started my gap year. From January to March 2019 I was an intern at F&S Solar in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic. This internship was followed by a stay in Israel from April to June 2019. There I first assisted research on graphene under the guidance of Michael Moshe at the Racah Institute of Physics in Jerusalem. Then I did an internship at Houlihan Lokey in Tel Aviv from July to September 2019.
In September 2019 I started studying physics in Zurich.
Besides learning about physics and mathematics, I am a very passionate golfer (HCI: 6.7) and I produce my own music which I publish on SoundCloud.
title: About

content:
The Ultimate Way To Read Textbooks Repeat for all chapters:
Flip through each page in the chapter (yields info about what to look for, the structure and other subconscious benefits) Read the questions at the end of the chapter (Baader-Meinhof Phenomenon) Read bold words in the chapter (titles, subtitles, subsubtitles) to get an even better understanding of the structure Read the first and last sentence of each paragraph (first sentence gives an indication on what the rest of the paragraph is about; the last sentence sums up the important information) Read through the chapter and take notes (brings everything together and lets you connect the dots)
title: The Ultimate Way To Read Textbooks
